title: Ulmus campestris lusus pumila
taxonomy: Ulmus campestris lusus pumila is a cultivar of Ulmus campestris, commonly known as the field elm. It is a dwarf or compact variety, often grown as an ornamental tree due to its smaller size. The classification of Ulmus campestris lusus pumila can be attributed to its botanical characteristics and growth habits. As a cultivar, it is not a naturally occurring species, but rather a selectively bred version of the field elm. Its classification is often used in horticulture and arboriculture to identify this specific variety of the field elm.

intro: The Ulmus campestris lusus pumila is a dwarf or stunted form of the field elm tree (Ulmus campestris), a deciduous tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects that can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Ulmus campestris lusus pumila include flavonoids, such as isorhapontigenin and rhaponticin, as well as oligostilbenes like vescalagin and isovescalagin. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
